client_like.h(3) Library Functions Manual client_like.h(3)

NAME

client_like.h - Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_client_like_config

Typedefs


typedef enum session_client_like_kind session_client_like_kind_t
typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)
typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Enumerations


enum session_client_like_kind { SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR = 0, SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT, SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY }

Functions


terminal_fd_reader_t * session_client_like_get_stdin_reader (void)
asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *config)

Variables


bool(*)(void *) session_client_like_get_render_should_exit (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

Provides a unified interface for running client-like modes that share common initialization patterns: media source selection, FPS probing, audio initialization, display creation, splash screen management, keepawake handling, and cleanup.

Each mode registers mode-specific callbacks to handle initialization, the main render loop, and keyboard input. The shared layer handles all the boilerplate.

Modes Supported

  • Mirror mode: Local webcam/media playback without networking
  • Client mode: Network client with per-connection initialization
  • Discovery mode: P2P mode with role negotiation

Shared Responsibilities

This layer automatically handles:

  • Terminal output management (stdout piping detection)
  • Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
  • Splash screen lifecycle and animation
  • Media source selection and FPS probing
  • Audio initialization and lifecycle
  • Display context creation and management
  • Render loop execution
  • Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)

Mode-Specific Responsibilities

Mode files provide:

  • Main loop callback (run_fn) that calls session_render_loop() or custom loop
  • Optional network interrupt callback for socket shutdown
  • Optional custom exit condition for additional shutdown criteria
  • Keyboard handler for interactive controls
  • Terminal behavior preferences (newline on exit)

Memory and Lifecycle

All allocations (capture, display, audio) are owned and cleaned up by session_client_like_run(). Mode callbacks receive initialized, ready-to-use contexts and should not attempt to free them.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file client_like.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Configuration for session_client_like_run()

All media, display, audio, FPS, and snapshot settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the fields below that differ per mode.

typedef enum session_client_like_kind session_client_like_kind_t

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)

Mode-specific main loop callback

Called after all shared initialization is complete (capture opened, audio started, display ready, splash done). The mode runs its main loop here and returns when finished. The shared teardown runs after this returns.

Typical pattern:

// For mirror/discovery participant:
return session_render_loop(capture, display, exit_check_fn, NULL, NULL,


                           keyboard_handler, user_data);
// For client mode (protocol-driven):
while (!should_exit()) {


  // protocol loop...
}
return ASCIICHAT_OK;

Parameters

capture Initialized capture context. Never NULL.
display Initialized display context. Never NULL.
user_data User data passed through from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 98 of file client_like.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum session_client_like_kind

Enumerator

SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR
SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT
SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY

Definition at line 63 of file client_like.h.

63                                       {
64   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR = 0,
65   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
66   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
67 } session_client_like_kind_t;










Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const
  session_client_like_config_t * config)


Run a client-like mode with fully shared initialization and
    teardown




This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of client-like
    modes:








Initialization
  (automatic from GET_OPTION())


Terminal:


    
  
  • Forces stderr when stdout is piped (terminal_should_force_stderr)
    • 
  
  • Disables terminal logging during rendering, re-enables on exit
    • 
  
  • Handles snapshot mode log visibility
    • 



Keepawake:


    
  
  • Validates --keepawake / --no-keepawake mutual exclusivity
    • 
  
  • Calls platform_enable_keepawake() unless --no-keepawake set
    • 
  
  • Disables keepawake on exit
    • 



Splash Screen:


    
  
  • Creates temporary display context
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_start() to show splash animation
    • 
  
  • Holds splash visible during media probing and audio init
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_done() when capture and display are ready
    • 



Media / Webcam
    (session_capture_ctx_t):



  
•

  
Selects source type from options (priority order):





  
 1.

  
--url → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE (FFmpeg handles http/rtsp/rtmp)

  
 2.

  
--file → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE; "-" →
      MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN

  
 3.

  
--test-pattern → MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST

  
 4.

  
(default) → MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM




    
  
  • FPS: uses GET_OPTION(fps) if > 0, otherwise probes with
      temporary media source
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp) as initial seek
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_loop) for file looping
    • 
  
  • Calls session_capture_create() to open webcam or media file
    • 
  
  • Destroys capture on exit
    • 



Audio (audio_context_t):


    
  
  • Skipped entirely for immediate snapshots (snapshot_mode &&
      snapshot_delay == 0)
    • 
  
  • Probes for audio via media_source_has_audio() on capture's
    source
    • 
  
  • Allocates and initializes audio_context_t when audio available
    • 
  
  • Links audio context to capture and media source
    • 
  
  • Sets playback_only based on audio_should_enable_microphone()
    • 
  
  • Disables jitter buffering for local file playback
    • 
  
  • Calls audio_start_duplex() after splash_intro_done()
    • 
  
  • Stops, destroys, and frees audio on exit
    • 



Display (session_display_ctx_t):


    
  
  • Calls session_display_create() with snapshot and color
    settings
    • 
  
  • Passes audio context if initialized
    • 
  
  • Destroys display on exit
    • 







Mode-Specific
  Loop


Calls config->run_fn(capture, display,
    config->run_user_data) once all initialization is complete. This is where
    each mode calls session_render_loop() or runs its own
    protocol-thread-driven loop.








Cleanup
  (always runs, even on error)


Cleanup sequence (order is critical):



  
1.

  
Re-enable terminal logging

  
2.

  
audio_terminate_portaudio_final() - Free PortAudio device resources
      FIRST

  
3.

  
audio_stop_duplex() + audio_destroy() + SAFE_FREE

  
4.

  
session_display_destroy()

  
5.

  
session_capture_destroy()

  
6.

  
webcam_destroy() - Free cached webcam images and test patterns

  
7.

  
session_log_buffer_destroy()

  
8.

  
platform_disable_keepawake()

  
9.

  
Print '
    

  




Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration.
  Must not be NULL. run_fn must not be NULL.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from
  initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return
  value.


Definition at line 95 of file client_like.c.


95                                                                                       {
96   log_info("session_client_like_run: ENTRY");
97   if (!config || !config->run_fn) {
98     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config or run_fn is NULL");
99   }
100   log_info("session_client_like_run: config validated");
101 
102   // Store config globally for adapter callbacks
103   g_current_config = config;
104 
105   // Store the render should_exit adapter globally so mode-specific run_fn can access it
106   g_render_should_exit = display_should_exit_adapter;
107 
108   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
109 
110   // Keep track of what's been initialized for cleanup
111   session_capture_ctx_t *capture = NULL;
112   session_display_ctx_t *display = NULL;
113   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = NULL;
114   media_source_t *probe_source = NULL;
115   bool audio_available = false;
116 
117   // ============================================================================
118   // SETUP: Terminal and Logging
119   // ============================================================================
120 
121   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up terminal and logging");
122 
123   // Force stderr when stdout is piped (prevents ASCII corruption in output stream)
124   bool should_force_stderr = terminal_should_force_stderr();
125   log_debug("terminal_should_force_stderr()=%d", should_force_stderr);
126 
127   if (should_force_stderr) {
128     // Redirect logs to stderr to prevent corruption of stdout (for pipes)
129     // But keep terminal output enabled so we can see initialization errors
130     log_set_force_stderr(true);
131   }
132 
133   // Note: Force all logging to stderr if encoding to pipe to prevent corrupting binary video output
134   // (implemented via should_force_stderr check above)
135   const char *render_file_early = GET_OPTION(render_file);
136   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_RUN] render_file_early=%s (ptr=%p)",
137            render_file_early ? render_file_early : "(null)", (void *)render_file_early);
138   if (render_file_early && render_file_early[0] != '\0' &&
139       (strcmp(render_file_early, "-") == 0 || strcmp(render_file_early, "pipe:") == 0)) {
140     log_info("--render-file=- will output binary video to stdout, logging sent to stderr");
141   }
142 
143   // ============================================================================
144   // SETUP: Keepawake System
145   // ============================================================================
146 
147   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Validating keepawake options");
148 
149   // Validate mutual exclusivity
150   bool enable_ka = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
151   bool disable_ka = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
152   log_debug("enable_keepawake=%d, disable_keepawake=%d", enable_ka, disable_ka);
153 
154   if (enable_ka && disable_ka) {
155     result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
156     goto cleanup;
157   }
158 
159   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Enabling keepawake if needed");
160 
161   // Enable keepawake unless explicitly disabled
162   if (!disable_ka) {
163     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
164   }
165 
166   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Keepawake setup complete");
167 
168   // ============================================================================
169   // SETUP: Display Context (before splash for unified TTY initialization)
170   // ============================================================================
171 
172   // Create display context early to share TTY initialization between splash and rendering
173   // This prevents double TTY init which was corrupting the terminal state
174   session_display_config_t display_config = {0};
175   display_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
176   display_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
177   display_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
178   display_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
179   display_config.should_exit_callback = display_should_exit_adapter;
180   display_config.callback_data = NULL;
181 
182   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Creating display context (before splash)");
183   display = session_display_create(&display_config);
184   if (!display) {
185     log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] session_display_create() returned NULL");
186     log_fatal("Failed to initialize display context");
187     result = ERROR_DISPLAY;
188     goto cleanup;
189   }
190   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Display context created, will use for splash");
191 
192   // Register global display context for signal handlers (Ctrl+C help screen closure)
193   // This makes the context available to signal handlers for the entire app lifetime
194   session_display_set_global_context(display);
195 
196   // ============================================================================
197   // SETUP: Splash Screen (using unified display context)
198   // ============================================================================
199 
200   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Starting splash with display context");
201 
202   // Use the display context we just created for splash animation
203   splash_intro_start(display);
204   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): splash_intro_start() returned");
205 
206   // Detect if we're using media vs webcam (needed for splash timing)
207   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
208   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
209   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
210 
211   // Show splash briefly for webcam, but skip sleep for media (which takes time anyway)
212   if (!has_media && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
213     APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
214     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, 250);
215   }
216   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): After splash sleep");
217 
218   // Restore stderr now that splash animation and post-splash logging are done
219   // This allows logs to appear on screen again after this point
220   splash_restore_stderr();
221 
222   // ============================================================================
223   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
224   // ============================================================================
225 
226   // ============================================================================
227   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
228   // ============================================================================
229 
230   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): About to disable terminal logging (snapshot=%d, discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
231   // For discovery mode, skip disabling logging initially - we'll handle it after role is determined
232   // Discovery participants should show logs, only hosts should have logs disabled for rendering
233   bool should_disable_logging = !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && (config->discovery == NULL);
234   if (should_disable_logging) {
235     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Calling log_set_terminal_output(false)...");
236     log_set_terminal_output(false);
237     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): RETURNED from log_set_terminal_output(false)");
238   } else {
239     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Skipping log disable (snapshot=%d or discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
240   }
241   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Terminal logging setup complete, starting media source setup");
242 
243   // ============================================================================
244   // SETUP: Media Source Selection and FPS Probing
245   // ============================================================================
246 
247   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Initializing capture config");
248   session_capture_config_t capture_config = {0};
249   capture_config.resize_for_network = false;
250   capture_config.should_exit_callback = capture_should_exit_adapter;
251   capture_config.callback_data = NULL;
252 
253   // Determine FPS explicitly set by user
254   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting FPS option");
255   int user_fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
256   bool fps_explicitly_set = user_fps > 0;
257   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): FPS=%d (explicitly_set=%d)", user_fps, fps_explicitly_set);
258 
259   // Select media source based on options (priority order)
260   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_url option");
261   const char *media_url_val = GET_OPTION(media_url);
262   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_file option");
263   const char *media_file_val = GET_OPTION(media_file);
264   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): media_url=%s, media_file=%s", media_url_val ? media_url_val : "(null)",
265             media_file_val ? media_file_val : "(null)");
266 
267   if (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) {
268     // Network URL takes priority
269     log_info("Using network URL: %s (webcam disabled)", media_url_val);
270     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
271     capture_config.path = media_url_val;
272     capture_config.loop = false; // Network URLs cannot be looped
273 
274     if (fps_explicitly_set) {
275       capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
276       log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
277     } else {
278       // Always probe FPS for HTTP URLs, even in snapshot mode
279       // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
280       log_debug("Probing FPS for HTTP URL");
281       probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_url_val);
282       if (probe_source) {
283         double url_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
284         log_info("Detected HTTP stream video FPS: %.1f", url_fps);
285         if (url_fps > 0.0) {
286           capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(url_fps + 0.5);
287         } else {
288           log_warn("FPS detection failed for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
289           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
290         }
291       } else {
292         log_warn("Failed to create probe source for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
293         capture_config.target_fps = 60;
294       }
295     }
296   } else if (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) {
297     // Local file or stdin
298     log_info("DEBUG: media_file_val='%s', strcmp result=%d", media_file_val, strcmp(media_file_val, "-"));
299     if (strcmp(media_file_val, "-") == 0) {
300       log_info("Using stdin for media streaming (webcam disabled)");
301       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN;
302       capture_config.path = NULL;
303       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
304       capture_config.loop = false;
305     } else {
306       log_info("Using media file: %s (webcam disabled)", media_file_val);
307       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
308       capture_config.path = media_file_val;
309       capture_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop);
310 
311       if (fps_explicitly_set) {
312         capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
313         log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
314       } else {
315         // Always probe FPS for local files, even in snapshot mode
316         // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
317         log_debug("Probing FPS for local file");
318         probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_file_val);
319         if (probe_source) {
320           double file_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
321           log_info("Detected file video FPS: %.1f", file_fps);
322           if (file_fps > 0.0) {
323             capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(file_fps + 0.5);
324           } else {
325             log_warn("FPS detection failed, using default 60 FPS");
326             capture_config.target_fps = 60;
327           }
328         } else {
329           log_warn("Failed to create probe source for FPS detection, using default 60 FPS");
330           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
331         }
332       }
333     }
334   } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
335     // Test pattern
336     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Using test pattern");
337     log_info("Using test pattern");
338     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
339     capture_config.path = NULL;
340     capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
341     capture_config.loop = false;
342   } else {
343     // Default: Try webcam if stdout is a TTY, otherwise use test pattern
344     // (webcam + piped output often fails, test pattern is reliable fallback)
345     bool stdout_is_tty = terminal_is_stdout_tty();
346     if (stdout_is_tty) {
347       // Normal terminal mode: use webcam
348       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is TTY, using local webcam");
349       log_info("Using local webcam");
350       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
351       capture_config.path = NULL;
352       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
353       capture_config.loop = false;
354     } else {
355       // Piped output mode: use test pattern (webcam + pipes often fails)
356       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is piped, defaulting to test pattern for reliability");
357       log_info("Stdout is piped - using test pattern instead of webcam");
358       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
359       capture_config.path = NULL;
360       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
361       capture_config.loop = false;
362     }
363   }
364 
365   // Apply initial seek if specified
366   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_seek_timestamp option");
367   capture_config.initial_seek_timestamp = GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp);
368   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Media seek timestamp set");
369 
370   // Clean up probe source (don't reuse for actual capture)
371   if (probe_source) {
372     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Destroying probe source");
373     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
374     probe_source = NULL;
375     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Probe source destroyed");
376   } else {
377     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): No probe source to destroy");
378   }
379 
380   // Pass probed FPS to display context for correct render-file duration
381   if (capture_config.target_fps > 0) {
382     session_display_set_render_fps(display, capture_config.target_fps);
383     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Set display render FPS to %u for correct video encoding duration",
384               capture_config.target_fps);
385   }
386 
387   // ============================================================================
388   // SETUP: Network Transports (TCP/WebSocket)
389   // ============================================================================
390 
391   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up network transports");
392 
393   bool networked_mode = session_client_like_is_networked(config->kind);
394 
395   switch (config->kind) {
396     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR:
397       log_debug("Mirror mode detected - will use local capture with media source");
398       break;
399     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT:
400       log_debug("Client/Network mode detected - will use network capture without local media source");
401       break;
402     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY:
403       log_debug("Discovery mode detected - discovery session will manage networking");
404       break;
405   }
406 
407   // ============================================================================
408   // SETUP: Capture Context
409   // ============================================================================
410 
411   // Check for stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, render to video
412   // Only enable stdin render mode if:
413   // 1. render-file is "-" (output to stdout)
414   // 2. stdin is not a TTY
415   // 3. --height is explicitly specified (user must opt-in to stdin render mode)
416   // 4. NO media source is being used (not reading from file/URL/test-pattern)
417   // This prevents accidental activation when piping to other processes or using mirror mode
418   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
419   int frame_height = GET_OPTION(height);
420   bool height_explicitly_set = (frame_height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT);
421   bool has_media_source = (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) ||
422                           (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) ||
423                           GET_OPTION(test_pattern);
424 
425   // Enable audio capture if render-file is active and media source exists
426   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0 && has_media_source) {
427     capture_config.enable_audio = true;
428     log_debug("Audio capture enabled for render-file encoding");
429   }
430 
431   bool stdin_render_mode = (render_file_opt && strcmp(render_file_opt, "-") == 0 &&
432                             !terminal_is_stdin_tty() && height_explicitly_set &&
433                             !has_media_source && config->kind != SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR);
434 
435   if (stdin_render_mode) {
436     // Stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, output video to stdout
437     log_info("Stdin render mode enabled: reading ASCII frames from stdin, output to stdout");
438 
439     // Get frame dimensions (--height must be explicitly set to reach here)
440     int frame_width = GET_OPTION(width);
441 
442     // Validate stdin render mode constraints (--height is already validated above since height_explicitly_set is true)
443     bool width_was_explicitly_set = (frame_width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT);
444 
445     if (width_was_explicitly_set) {
446       result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Stdin render mode does not accept --width (auto-detected from frames).\n"
447                                       "Only specify: --height <rows>");
448       goto cleanup;
449     }
450 
451     asciichat_error_t stdin_err = terminal_fd_reader_create(STDIN_FILENO, frame_height, &g_stdin_reader);
452     if (stdin_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
453       log_fatal("Failed to initialize stdin frame reader: %s", asciichat_error_string(stdin_err));
454       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
455       goto cleanup;
456     }
457 
458     // For stdin render mode, create a minimal capture context for compatibility
459     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
460     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
461     if (!capture) {
462       log_fatal("Failed to initialize capture context for stdin rendering");
463       terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
464       g_stdin_reader = NULL;
465       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
466       goto cleanup;
467     }
468 
469     log_info("stdin render mode: reading %d-line frames from stdin, auto-detecting width", frame_height);
470   }
471 
472   // Choose capture type based on mode determined earlier:
473   // - Mirror mode: needs to capture local media (webcam, file, test pattern)
474   // - Network modes (client/discovery): receive frames from network, no local capture
475   if (!stdin_render_mode && networked_mode) {
476     // Network mode: create minimal capture context without media source
477     log_debug("Network mode detected - using network capture (no local media source)");
478     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
479     uint64_t net_cap_start = time_get_ns();
480     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
481     double net_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(net_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
482     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Network capture created in %.1f ms", net_cap_ms);
483     if (!capture) {
484       log_fatal("Failed to initialize network capture context");
485       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
486       goto cleanup;
487     }
488     if (fps > 0) {
489       log_debug("Network capture FPS set to %d from options", fps);
490     }
491   }
492 
493   // ============================================================================
494   // SETUP: Audio Context
495   // ============================================================================
496 
497   // Skip audio for immediate snapshots
498   bool should_init_audio = true;
499   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0) {
500     should_init_audio = false;
501     log_debug("Skipping audio initialization for immediate snapshot");
502   }
503 
504   // Probe for audio
505   if (should_init_audio && capture_config.type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && capture_config.path) {
506     media_source_t *audio_probe_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
507     if (audio_probe_source && media_source_has_audio(audio_probe_source)) {
508       audio_available = true;
509 
510       // Allocate and initialize audio context
511       audio_ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(audio_context_t), audio_context_t *);
512       if (audio_ctx) {
513         *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t){0};
514         if (audio_init(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
515           // Link audio to media source
516           media_source_t *media_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
517           audio_ctx->media_source = media_source;
518 
519           if (media_source) {
520             media_source_set_audio_context(media_source, audio_ctx);
521           }
522 
523           // Determine if microphone should be enabled
524           bool should_enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), audio_available);
525           audio_ctx->playback_only = !should_enable_mic;
526 
527           // Disable jitter buffering for file playback
528           if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
529             audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_enabled = false;
530             atomic_store_bool(&audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
531           }
532 
533           // Store in capture for keyboard handler access
534           session_capture_set_audio_context(capture, audio_ctx);
535 
536           log_debug("Audio context initialized");
537         } else {
538           log_warn("Failed to initialize audio context");
539           audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
540           SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
541           audio_ctx = NULL;
542           audio_available = false;
543         }
544       }
545     }
546   }
547 
548   // Pass stdin_reader to display if in stdin render mode
549   if (stdin_render_mode && g_stdin_reader) {
550     session_display_set_stdin_reader(display, g_stdin_reader);
551     log_debug("stdin_reader passed to display context");
552   }
553 
554   // ============================================================================
555   // SETUP: End Splash Screen
556   // ============================================================================
557   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] About to end splash screen");
558 
559   // The splash screen will display during initialization and exit when done.
560   // The initialization process (media probing, display setup, etc.) naturally
561   // takes time, keeping the splash visible. The splash is kept alive during
562   // connection attempts (TCP and WebSocket) to prevent a blank screen during
563   // network operations. splash_intro_done() is called only after a successful
564   // connection is established.
565 
566   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Splash will remain visible during connection attempts");
567 
568   // Exit early if shutdown was requested (e.g., user pressed Ctrl-C)
569   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
570     log_debug("[SETUP] Shutdown requested, exiting early");
571     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Ending splash due to early shutdown request");
572     splash_intro_done();
573     splash_wait_for_animation();
574     result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
575     goto cleanup;
576   }
577 
578   // Wait for splash animation to finish before proceeding to render loop
579   // (splash_anim thread uses the display, must not be disrupted before cleanup)
580   // But skip for immediate snapshots (snapshot-delay=0) which need to output first frame immediately
581   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
582   if (config->discovery == NULL) {
583     splash_intro_done();
584     if (!(GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0)) {
585       splash_wait_for_animation();
586     }
587   } else {
588     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Discovery mode: keeping splash visible while waiting for peers");
589   }
590 
591   // ============================================================================
592   // SETUP: Create Mirror Capture (after splash screen finishes)
593   // ============================================================================
594 
595   log_info("[MIRROR_CHECK] stdin_render_mode=%d, capture=%p", stdin_render_mode, (void *)capture);
596   if (!stdin_render_mode && !capture) {
597     // Mirror mode: create capture context with local media source (NOW, after splash finishes)
598     log_info("Mirror mode detected - creating mirror capture after splash screen");
599     uint64_t mirror_cap_start = time_get_ns();
600     capture = session_mirror_capture_create(&capture_config);
601     double mirror_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(mirror_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
602     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Mirror capture created in %.1f ms (after splash)", mirror_cap_ms);
603     if (!capture) {
604       log_fatal("Failed to initialize mirror capture source");
605       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
606       goto cleanup;
607     }
608 
609     // Set audio source for render-file encoding (after capture is created)
610     media_source_t *render_audio_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
611     const char *render_file_path = GET_OPTION(render_file);
612     log_info("[AUDIO_SOURCE] render_audio_source=%p, render_file=%s, render_file_len=%zu",
613              render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
614              render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
615     if (render_audio_source && render_file_path && strlen(render_file_path) > 0) {
616       session_display_set_render_audio_source(display, render_audio_source);
617       log_info("Audio source set for render-file output");
618     } else {
619       log_warn("[AUDIO_SOURCE] Conditions not met: render_audio_source=%p, render_file_path=%s, len=%zu",
620                render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
621                render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
622     }
623   }
624 
625   // ============================================================================
626   // SETUP: Start Audio Playback
627   // ============================================================================
628 
629   log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] About to check audio context for duplex");
630   if (audio_ctx && audio_available) {
631     log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] Starting audio duplex");
632     if (audio_start_duplex(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
633       log_info("Audio playback started");
634     } else {
635       log_warn("Failed to start audio duplex");
636       audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
637       SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
638       audio_ctx = NULL;
639       audio_available = false;
640     }
641   }
642 
643   log_info("[MARKER] Audio setup complete at line 614");
644   log_info("[SETUP_COMPLETE] All setup complete, about to start connection loop");
645 
646   // ============================================================================
647   // RUN: Mode-Specific Main Loop with Reconnection
648   // ============================================================================
649 
650   // Connection/attempt loop - wraps run_fn with reconnection logic
651   int attempt = 0;
652   int max_attempts = config->max_reconnect_attempts;
653 
654   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_LOOP] Starting connection loop, max_attempts=%d", max_attempts);
655 
656   while (true) {
657     attempt++;
658 
659     result = config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data);
660 
661     // Exit immediately if run_fn succeeded
662     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
663       // Connection succeeded - splash screen cleanup now happens when first frame renders
664       // This ensures the splash stays visible during TCP/WebSocket/datachannel connection attempts
665 
666       break;
667     }
668 
669     // Check if we should attempt reconnection
670     bool should_retry = false;
671 
672     if (max_attempts != 0) { // max_attempts == 0 means no retries
673       // Check custom reconnect logic if provided
674       if (config->should_reconnect_callback) {
675         should_retry = config->should_reconnect_callback(result, attempt, config->reconnect_user_data);
676       } else {
677         should_retry = true; // Default: always retry
678       }
679 
680       // Check retry limits if still planning to retry
681       if (should_retry && max_attempts > 0 && attempt >= max_attempts) {
682         log_debug("Reached maximum reconnection attempts (%d), giving up", max_attempts);
683         should_retry = false;
684       }
685     }
686 
687     // If not retrying or shutdown requested, exit loop
688     if (!should_retry || APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
689       break;
690     }
691 
692     // Reconnection will happen - show splash screen during reconnection attempt
693     // Reset first_frame flag so splash cleanup runs on next successful connection
694     session_display_reset_first_frame(display);
695 
696     // splash_intro_start() handles all the logic:
697     // - If splash should display: shows splash and disables console logging
698     // - If splash shouldn't display: restores stderr and returns without changing logging state
699     splash_intro_start(display);
700 
701     // Log reconnection message
702     if (max_attempts == -1) {
703       log_info("Connection failed, retrying...");
704     } else if (max_attempts > 0) {
705       log_info("Connection failed, retrying (attempt %d/%d)...", attempt + 1, max_attempts);
706     }
707 
708     // Apply reconnection delay if configured
709     // Check APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit) frequently during sleep so SIGTERM can interrupt reconnection attempts
710     if (config->reconnect_delay_ms > 0) {
711       unsigned int remaining_ms = config->reconnect_delay_ms;
712       const unsigned int check_interval_ms = 100; // Check exit flag every 100ms
713 
714       while (remaining_ms > 0 && !APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
715         unsigned int sleep_ms = (remaining_ms < check_interval_ms) ? remaining_ms : check_interval_ms;
716         APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
717         APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, sleep_ms);
718         remaining_ms -= sleep_ms;
719       }
720     }
721 
722     // Continue loop to retry
723   }
724 
725   // ============================================================================
726   // CLEANUP (always runs, even on error)
727   // ============================================================================
728 
729 cleanup:
730   // Re-enable terminal output for shutdown logs
731   log_set_terminal_output(true);
732 
733   g_current_config = NULL;
734   g_render_should_exit = NULL;
735 
736   // CRITICAL: Terminate PortAudio device resources FIRST
737   log_debug("Terminating PortAudio device resources");
738   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
739 
740   // Stop audio thread before destroying audio context to prevent use-after-free
741   // The audio worker thread may still be logging when we destroy the buffer
742   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread);
743 
744   // Stop and destroy audio (after PortAudio is terminated and audio thread is stopped)
745   if (audio_ctx) {
746     audio_stop_duplex(audio_ctx);
747     audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
748     SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
749     audio_ctx = NULL;
750   }
751 
752   // Destroy display (check global context to see if discovery mode already destroyed it)
753   // Discovery participant mode may destroy the display early and clear the global context
754   session_display_ctx_t *current_global = session_display_get_global_context();
755   if (display && display == current_global) {
756     // Display is still valid (not destroyed by discovery mode)
757     session_display_destroy(display);
758     display = NULL;
759   } else if (display && display != current_global) {
760     // Display was already destroyed (by discovery participant mode)
761     // Just NULL it out, don't call destroy again
762     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Display was already destroyed by discovery mode, skipping destroy");
763     display = NULL;
764   }
765 
766   // Destroy capture
767   if (capture) {
768     session_capture_destroy(capture);
769     capture = NULL;
770   }
771 
772   // Destroy stdin reader if active (stdin render mode)
773   if (g_stdin_reader) {
774     terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
775     g_stdin_reader = NULL;
776   }
777 
778   // Clean up probe source if still allocated (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
779   if (probe_source) {
780     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
781     probe_source = NULL;
782   }
783 
784   // Stop splash animation and enforce minimum display time (even on error path)
785   // But skip completely if shutting down - don't interact with splash at all during shutdown
786   // The animation thread will exit on its own when it detects shutdown_is_requested()
787   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
788   if (config->discovery != NULL) {
789     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Discovery mode: keeping splash alive for peer waiting UI");
790   } else if (!APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
791     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_intro_done()");
792     splash_intro_done();
793     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_intro_done() returned");
794 
795     // Wait for animation thread to exit before cleanup
796     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_wait_for_animation()");
797     splash_wait_for_animation();
798     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_wait_for_animation() returned");
799   } else {
800     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Skipping all splash operations (shutdown in progress)");
801     // During shutdown, don't interact with splash - let animation thread exit naturally
802     // and don't wait for it (prevents blocking on signals)
803   }
804 
805   // Stop debug sync thread before destroying log buffer to prevent use-after-free
806   log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call debug_sync_cleanup_thread()");
807   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
808   log_debug("[CLEANUP] debug_sync_cleanup_thread() returned");
809 
810   // Disable keepawake (re-allow OS to sleep)
811   log_debug("[CLEANUP] Disabling keepawake");
812   platform_disable_keepawake();
813   log_debug("[CLEANUP] keepawake disabled");
814 
815   // Write newline to separate final frame from prompt (if configured)
816   log_debug("About to check print_newline_on_tty_exit");
817   if (config->print_newline_on_tty_exit && terminal_is_stdout_tty()) {
818     log_debug("Writing newline");
819     const char newline = '\n';
820     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, &newline, 1);
821   }
822 
823   log_set_terminal_output(false);
824 
825   return result;
826 }






References APP_CALLBACK_BOOL, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), audio_destroy(), audio_init(),
    audio_should_enable_microphone(), audio_start_duplex(),
    audio_stop_duplex(), audio_stop_thread(),
    audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::color_mode,
    session_display_config_t::custom_palette,
    debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    session_client_like_config::discovery,
    session_capture_config_t::enable_audio, ERROR_DISPLAY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_enabled,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_filled,
    session_client_like_config::kind, log_debug, log_fatal,
    log_info, log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_warn,
    session_capture_config_t::loop,
    session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts,
    audio_context_t::media_source, media_source_create(),
    media_source_destroy(), MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE,
    media_source_get_video_fps(), media_source_has_audio(),
    media_source_set_audio_context(), MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN,
    MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST, MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
    OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT, OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT,
    session_display_config_t::palette_type,
    session_capture_config_t::path, platform_disable_keepawake(),
    platform_enable_keepawake(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_write_all(), audio_context_t::playback_buffer,
    audio_context_t::playback_only,
    session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_user_data,
    session_capture_config_t::resize_for_network,
    session_client_like_config::run_fn,
    session_client_like_config::run_user_data, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, session_capture_destroy(),
    session_capture_get_media_source(),
    session_capture_set_audio_context(),
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR, session_display_create(),
    session_display_destroy(),
    session_display_get_global_context(),
    session_display_reset_first_frame(),
    session_display_set_global_context(),
    session_display_set_render_audio_source(),
    session_display_set_render_fps(),
    session_display_set_stdin_reader(),
    session_mirror_capture_create(),
    session_network_capture_create(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_capture_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_display_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback,
    session_display_config_t::snapshot_mode, splash_intro_done(),
    splash_intro_start(), splash_restore_stderr(),
    splash_wait_for_animation(),
    session_capture_config_t::target_fps, TERM_COLOR_AUTO,
    terminal_fd_reader_create(), terminal_fd_reader_destroy(),
    terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty(),
    terminal_should_force_stderr(), time_elapsed_ns(),
    time_get_ns(), and session_capture_config_t::type.


Referenced by client_main(), discovery_main(), and
    mirror_main().








Variable
  Documentation




bool(*)(void *)
  session_client_like_get_render_should_exit(void) (void )


Get the render loop should_exit callback set by
    session_client_like_run().




This is used by mode-specific run_fn callbacks to obtain the
    proper should_exit callback for passing to session_render_loop(). The
    callback checks both the global should_exit() flag and any
    mode-specific custom_should_exit condition.




Returns


Function pointer to the render should_exit callback, or
  NULL if session_client_like_run() has not been called yet.


Definition at line 245 of file client_like.h.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
